content.text:
The issue was a missing space between the "To" prefix and the recipient addresses in the "Sent" folder. This occurred in the `getRecipientDisplayNames` method of `MessageHelper.kt`, where the prefix (e.g., "To:") was directly appended to the recipients list without an intervening space.

I have fixed this by adding `.append(' ')` between the prefix and the recipients in `MessageHelper.kt`. I also added a new unit test file `MessageHelperRecipientTest.kt` to verify the fix and prevent regressions.

### Changes:
- Modified `legacy/core/src/main/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/MessageHelper.kt`:
    - Updated `getRecipientDisplayNames` to append a space after the display name prefix.
- Created `legacy/core/src/test/java/com/fsck/k9/helper/MessageHelperRecipientTest.kt`:
    - Added a unit test to verify that `getRecipientDisplayNames` correctly includes a space after the prefix.

### Verification:
- Ran the new unit test `MessageHelperRecipientTest`, which failed before the fix and passed after the fix.
- Verified that other parts of the codebase (like quoting) already handle spaces manually, confirming that the prefix from `CoreResourceProvider` is intended to be space-less.
